task_intent: Build the MemoryStore boundary and memory selection/application proof.

selected_context.reason: Reject repo-local memory-core claims and require selected memory IDs, rejected context, application guidance, and feedback.

rejected_context.reason: Expanded benchmark goals are lab/archive context and are rejected for default product work.
Full memory-bank scans are context dumps; this run may use only selected memory IDs.

required_skills.name: goal-execplan
required_skills.reason: The brief is an execution contract for a restartable Codex run.

required_skills.name: typescript-contract-engineer
required_skills.reason: The task touches TypeScript contracts, parsers, CLI, or package boundaries.

memory_selection.task_intent: Build the MemoryStore boundary and memory selection/application proof.

selected.reason: Selected because the task targets repo-local memory-core and context-dump failure modes.
selected.expected_use: Reject repo-local memory-core claims and require selected memory IDs, rejected context, application guidance, and feedback.

rejected_context.reason: Expanded benchmark goals are lab/archive context and are rejected for default product work.
Full memory-bank scans are context dumps; this run may use only selected memory IDs.

retrieval_budget.selection_policy: active planning records only; reject stale/lab/archive context and full memory-bank scans

memory_selection.overclaim_boundary: This selection proves bounded local-dev memory selection only.

blockers: Do not treat docs/memory/** or .krn/** as final memory core.
required_checks: Brief output must cite selected memory IDs and rejected context.
review_questions: Did selected memory change the next action?
memory_application.next_action: Use this application guidance in krn brief, then record pending feedback to the MemoryStore adapter.
memory_application.overclaim_boundary: This application proves operating guidance wiring only.

memory_outcomes.reason: KRN applied this memory to operating guidance; human/metric outcome is pending.

memory_feedback.notes: Feedback is usage evidence, not a productivity-lift claim.

next_action: Use this application guidance in krn brief, then record pending feedback to the MemoryStore adapter.
overclaim_boundary: This operating brief proves bounded memory selection/application for one local Codex run.
interpretation_caveat: This is runtime evidence for the next action, not authoritative memory core.
